root_cause.text: The ENCRYPT layer lacks message signing and verification to prevent replay attacks, allowing an attacker to intercept encrypted messages, increment their sequence numbers, and replay them to bypass encryption.
root_cause.evidence: The patch replaces ENCRYPT with SYM_ENCRYPT and ASYM_ENCRYPT, adding the option `sign_msgs` which appends "An encrypted checksum (signature) of the hash of the encrypted message... in the header" to prevent replay attacks, as described in `doc/design/MaliciousAttacks.txt`.

intent_record.intent_summary: Fix unintended shared reference modifications by storing a copy of the temporary map.
intent_record.what: Modified the put operation to insert a new TreeMap instantiated with the contents of 'tmp' instead of inserting the 'tmp' reference directly.
intent_record.how: Prevents subsequent modifications to the 'tmp' map from unintentionally altering the data already stored in 'retval' by creating a distinct copy.

intent_record.intent_summary: Refactor protocol insertion methods to use a strongly-typed Position enum instead of integer constants.
intent_record.what: Changed the 'position' parameter type from int to a Position enum in protocol insertion methods, and removed the corresponding runtime integer bounds check.
intent_record.how: By utilizing a strongly-typed enum, valid position values are now enforced at compile time, eliminating the need for manual runtime validation of integer constants.

intent_record.intent_summary: Refactor insertProtocol to use a Position enum instead of integer constants to improve type safety.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add null check to prevent NullPointerException in hashCode.
